Commander %str1%

Commander %str1%,Thanks to coordination with Delaqua Investigations, the paperwork involving...well, everything that happened on your big trip here has mostly been handled. Mostly. But we've still got grumblings from various ministry departments whose feathers were ruffled, and it would be a big help if you could drop by. Nothing to sign; just a few appearances so that MinSec can remind certain parties what an important VIP the commander is.It's not my business, but Min would really appreciate it. Which means it is my business a bit, and I'd take it as a big favor. Let's meet up in Arborstone.—Rama

Commander %str1%,The sun shines again today on Elona, on what I hope will be remembered as the beginning of an age of peace. We have you to thank for that—your battles against the god of war are still spoken of in the villages and halls.I write to you to ask you to work together with me again. The Sunspears are rebuilding, and we have more new recruits than I could have imagined. Seeing your courage and conviction would lend them strength. Help me show the people that we can stand as Elona's protectors once again.—Zaeim, Spearmarshal of the Sunspears

Commander %str1%,We have heard your request to speak with the Pale Tree. She has moments of clarity, but they're unpredictable and interspersed with long periods of unconsciousness. Please, come at your convenience, but you'll have to wait until she awakens.—Mender Aine of the Grove

Commander Varalyn thanks you for your aid.

Commander! Gorrik here. First, I just want to say how much I value our working relationship. We get a lot done, you and I, and I've always treasured that. I'm sure you have too, and I'd like you to keep that in mind as you move on to the next paragraph.The skyscale's gone. I was vaguely distracted by my research and looked away for the BRIEFEST moment and she's just...gone. Fortunately, I implanted a tracking device in her leg, but time is not on our side. Please return to Sun's Refuge so we can enact a plan.Respectfully yours,GorrikP.S. Please don't tell Taimi.
Commander! I'm glad you're here.

Commander! Please return to Dragonfall with all possible haste. Something strange is going on with the skyscales and I need your assistance. The necessary measures will involve getting close to the beasts, and...well, my attempts to do so have resulted in rather painful wounds. You forged a rapport with them, so you are uniquely qualified for the endeavor. Please hurry back to Dragonfall.—Gorrik

Commander! Your skyscale egg is stirring! I insist upon your presence for the birth! Taimi suggested liberal use of exclamation points to convey and inspire maximal excitement!!(!) Quickly now—hurry back to Sun's Refuge!
Commander!We could use your help. Like, REALLY use it. The Crystal Bloom is making preparations to deploy. Return to the Eye of the North for a briefing on our plans. OK? OK. Thanks!—Lokka

Commander, Great job collecting a new Ancient Boreal weapon. Due to your hard work I have discovered a new Restored Boreal weapon recipe for you! Come see me to purchase it.Sincerely,Anette Eymundrdottir

Commander, hello!Royalty from the Dominion of Winds has taken a keen interest in recent developments within the Tyrian Alliance—they are very impressed by your heroics in Janthir.As a gesture of good faith, an old friend of mine will be traveling to meet with you and other esteemed rangers from the alliance. As a fellow wanderer of the wilds, I imagine you'll have much to bond over. You can find him in Lion's Arch.Your friend,Kalidris

Commander, here's the route to the North Nolan Hatchery, where you can meet up with Rox, the recruit I mentioned. She's not in trouble, I just wanted to make sure you knew how appreciative I'd be if you did... Whatever this "Molten Alliance" is up to, I don't like it. I'd send more soldiers with her, but we're up to our fangs in refugees. Rox is capable, but I'm more confident knowing you've got her back.Exit the city via the northeastern gate that leads to Diessa Plateau. Pass through Nolan and head into the foothills. The hatchery is there.Your friend,Rytlock Brimstone

Commander, I pray this message finds you with all due haste.Our scouts report increased Destroyer activity around Ebonhawke. It's only a matter of time before the dragon of fire and destruction sets its sights on us.I know you have been spearheading Tyria's efforts to counter the ongoing Elder Dragon attacks. We fear Ebonhawke is next.—Captain Thaddeus Soren

Commander, I'm on the verge of another breakthrough! I still need some more time, but I'm getting close to unlocking an upgrade to the essence manipulation system that should push its abilities even further!I'll update you when I know more.

Commander, The end of Balthazar was the beginning of a new threat to our region. Please find enclosed a classified report prepared for your eyes only, using intelligence gained from confidential maginamic experts.—Kito

Commander, there's a rapidly developing situation in Lornar's Pass. Apparently, Scarlet Briar is deploying a massive construct based on the watchknights as some kind of sadistic field test.  The Priory is calling for immediate support, and a number of adventurers are already in the area battling Scarlet's armies. Mysterious probes have also started appearing all over the region.We could use your help, but after reading the reports from Kessex Hills I have to think this is the tip of a darker scheme. Stay on your toes, friend.—Logan
Commander, you forgot this in the Raven Sanctum.
Commander, you seem to have forgotten this.
Commander, you've found my journal. Meet me at what was once my village. It's near the base your allies established near the front lines. Or you can just follow the smell of ash.

Commander, your dedication, courage, and resourcefulness have not been exaggerated. The Order of Shadows thanks you for your aid. As the danger grows and our role moves from wary watchers to active defenders, any assistance for our cause is a gift eagerly accepted.You have made a difference here. May all of your endeavors be so successful.—Keeper Pamir

Commander,A disgruntled ex-employee, Sibaha, recently made off with several of our ranch's prized creatures.I've never agreed with her methods—she takes too many risks, and frankly, the shrinking demand has resulted in rising costs and dwindling skimmer populations. We just can't breed them like we used to. And I certainly can't afford to let her take a chunk of my remaining stock.Please help us find our skimmers. Our livelihoods depend on it!Find me at Skimmer Ranch here in Elon Riverlands.—Trainer Ardra

Commander,All over Cantha, scrying stones have been emerging... You may have noticed the spikes of power; the air is thicker in their presence, like sludge. They have a similar magical composition to the Scrying Pool in the Eye of the North. I can only assume that they function similarly, too, giving you the opportunity to relive battles and memories past... I implore you to explore them. Appease your curiosity. Appease mine.Do keep an eye out, and should you choose to approach them...bring friends. As many as you can find. The mind is a dangerous place to get lost.Stay on your toes.—Your Friend

Commander,Are you aware of our new paratrooper division? Our parachutes are military grade and offer increased mobility throughout Drizzlewood Coast. If we're going to win this war, need every advantage we can get.Come speak to me in Umbral Grotto.Over and out.

Commander,As I've stated many times, the Pact's victory over Zhaitan would not have been possible without you. I could confer upon you a host of richly deserved official commendations, but I'd rather offer my thanks as a friend and grateful peer. With that in mind, please accept this token—present it to Quartermaster Hargrove and he will offer you the pick of his finest weapons. Whatever you choose, know that it is a symbol of my personal gratitude and esteem. When the Pact next takes the field, I look forward to wielding Caladbolg alongside you and the weapon that commemorates our partnership.Your friend,—Trahearne

Commander,As we discussed, I'd like you to look into the problems Warmaster Dalia Caisson's artillery unit has been having near Mount Maelstrom. She's experienced a rash of missing personnel, lost supplies, and garbled orders. This is one of our best units and it occupies a key position, so that battery needs to function perfectly. Report to Caisson and see what you can do to help her solve her communication problems.—Marshal Trahearne

Commander,As we discussed, krait slavers have been taking anyone they can catch on or near the waters of Nonmoa Lake. These aren't just innocents, they're potential Pact recruits, so it's imperative that we free the prisoners and shut down the slavers. I've got Crusader Apatia, formerly of the Lionguard, standing by on the lakeshore. She remembers you from Claw Island and is quite keen to work with you again. Good luck.—Marshal Trahearne

Commander,As we discussed, we need to deal with the huge Orrian bone ship that's marauding along the coast of the Mire Sea. I want you to join our demolitions expert, an asura named Tonn. Assist him with his preparations and make sure the bone ship goes down. The Pact needs this victory, both to protect the coastline and to prove to potential allies and new recruits that we can stop the spread of Zhaitan's influence. Good luck.—Marshal Trahearne

Commander,Bad news about the demolitions expert. I suppose it's cold comfort to remind you that heroes die during wartime. Probably won't do any good to tell your Pact superiors that, either, but it's true.The important thing is that you got the job done.Destiny's Edge is still trying to make a go of it, but they're trying without me for now. I've had enough of childish accusations. I'd rather work with soldiers who understand their role, and the stakes.I'll be in touch.—Rytlock

Commander,Hack Strawfang here. And I've got something to show you!It's just a little invention I've been working on between battles. I call it...the medizooka. See, it's a bazooka, but when you shoot someone with it, it heals them! Hence, the "medi." But let's not get caught up in the linguistics of the thing. It's a new addition to the waystation, and a fine one, if I say so myself.If you wanna take a peek at it, I'm out at the Forward Camp in Legion's Alcove. Stop by anytime.—Strawfang

Commander,Here are the known White Mantle hideouts that the Shining Blade continue to monitor for activity. Though none of our spies reported any noteworthy activity the past few months, that doesn't mean that our three White Mantle wouldn't have left ties to their actions at any of the locations. At minimum, it's worth getting a closer look.Divinity's Reach: In the past, Shining Blade agents have observed White Mantle activity by the gardens in the northeastern corner of Rurikton. As it is near the gates leading to Lake Doric, it continues to be a notorious spot for White Mantle vs. Seraph altercations.Brisban Wildlands: Their hideout north of this region remains abandoned after we cleaned out one of their cells during that whole Lazarus adventure. However, clusters of the bandits in the Wendon Steps continue to be sympathetic to the White Mantle. They may still harbor the occasional justiciar and their forces. Couldn't hurt to take a look.Shire of Beetletun: In the northeast corner of Queensdale, the shire situated right outside Caudecus's former estate remains under the Shining Blade's watchful eye. Still, the minister's most loyal servants make pilgrimages to the site, perhaps hoping that against all hope that he may someday return. Pathetic, really... But then again, here we are working with a wizard returned from death. Who are we to judge what's possible? At any rate, I could see any of our three loyalists wanting to remain close to Caudecus this way.Exactly what should you be looking for at these locations? That, I leave to your judgment... A "you'll know it when you see it" sort of thing. Best of luck, Commander.

Commander,Hope this letter finds you in moderately higher spirits!Taimi, Yao, and I have been hard at work searching for an answer to our demon problem. And we think we've found a viable solution!A word of caution: our strategy will involve an exhaustive exploration of your mind's response to intense emotional stimuli. It will be...taxing.If anyone has my full confidence, it's you, Commander.When you're ready, come by the power plant in New Kaineng City to discuss our new plan of attack.—Gorrik

Commander,I am Captain Thaddeus Soren of the Ebon Vanguard. I pray this message finds you with all due haste.Our scouts are reporting increased destroyer activity all around Ebonhawke. I fear it's only a matter of time before the dragon of fire and destruction sets its sights on us.I know you have been spearheading Tyria's efforts to counter the ongoing Elder Dragon attacks. We fear Ebonhawke is next.—Captain Soren

Commander,I apologize for delaying our conversation, but I wanted to respect the difficult emotions surrounding your experience in the Mists. However, time is of the essence if we're to prepare Aurene and ourselves for the coming storm. Meet me at Sun's Refuge, and we can discuss our next steps.—Caithe

Commander,I hadn't planned on contacting you, but the situation requires it. We both are united in resisting the dragon threat. Inseparably so. To that end, we need help. Please head to the Eye of the North and give whatever aid you can.—Dai

Commander,I heard about the loss of your demolitionist pal Tonn. I didn't know him, but I think I might have gotten along with him—people who like to blow stuff up tend to keep the conversation lively. Try not to let his death ruin the rest of your war, and keep up the good work.I think Eir is plotting something to smooth things over between me and her, or me and the rest of Destiny's edge. I may see what she's got in mind, just for a laugh. I'll let you know how it goes.Until then,—Zojja

Commander,I heard about the loss of your Lionguard friend Apatia. She sounds like a norn woman I might have gotten along with, which would be a pleasant change of pace at this juncture. Try not to let her death ruin the rest of your war, and keep up the good work.Speaking of norn women, I think Eir is plotting something to smooth things over between me and her, or me and the rest of Destiny's edge. I may see what she's got in mind, just for a laugh. I'll let you know how it goes. Until then,—Zojja
Commander,I heard something happened with the artifact! Come meet me back at the Durmand Priory camp so we can figure out what it was.

Commander,I know you're occupied with your pursuit of Inquest forces further inland, but if you have a spare moment, I could do with another expert pair of eyes on an escalating situation in Shipwreck Strand. I can say no more in writing; please, if you're able to help, meet me on the cliffs overlooking the Dark Leviathan, just south of the waypoint.The wind at your back,—Kestrel Choole, Alliance Representative of Mori Village

Commander,I know you've been very busy, what with the charr insurrections and killing of Elder Dragons, but I think you might want to pay a visit to the Eye of the North. Something seems to be bothering Aurene.It has been too long. I'm looking forward to seeing you again.—C

Commander,I watched your interaction with the Inquest at a distance. I'll be sending another scout to take the surviving asura into alliance custody and get them patched up. When you're ready, meet me back at the hunter's camp; we should discuss next steps.—Choole
Commander,I wish to discuss an important matter with you. Meet me at your home as soon as possible.—Ridhais

Commander,I've arranged for you to meet with a former colleague of mine in Rata Sum—a great mind, until my sister poached him for Dynamics.He's come upon a curious discovery. I suspect it may aid you in your ventures, or at least serve as a "fun" conversational anecdote.Regards,Councillor Ludo

Commander,I've read the early dispatches from your mission, and the krait orb is definitely something I want to examine. I'm preparing a team to study and deploy the object—get it and yourself back to Fort Trinity as soon as you can: Zhaitan's forces are planning a major assault on our headquarters, and I want the Pact to be ready. Until then,—Marshal Trahearne

Commander,I've recovered an artifact that is acting very strangely when exposed to different kinds of magic. I've got a favor to ask of you that might help figure out what's going on. Come find me when you can; I'm at the Durmand Priory camp in Starlit Weald.

Commander,In the lull after we shut down Fahranur, Rytlock and I began to worry that Joko's invasion attempts may be the first step in a larger plan. We've decided to quietly begin investigating for any intel that might shed light on his future intentions. Rytlock is focusing his efforts on Istan, while I have been reaching out to various parties here in Amnoon.By the way, Rytlock tells me our corsair friend, Sayida the Sly, used all that bounty money to get her hands on an airship. That may come in handy—assuming she sees an opportunity to enhance her reputation or wealth, of course.Please do get in touch if you need us.—Canach

Commander,In the process of setting up your Legendary Armory, this item was unable to return to your inventory. Please ensure that you have the free inventory space to reclaim it. If it is in excess of what the armory allows, please contact Customer Service to exchange it.

Commander,It's been a while. I wish I were writing you with better news, but times are difficult. The Olmakhan are committed to battle against the dragons wherever the fight takes us. Return to the Eye and help however you can.—Rox

Commander,Jory and I have something we want to share with all our dearest friends. Please join us in Divinity's Reach, at the spot where we first met. Don't bring anything but your wonderful self. We're looking forward to seeing you and having this chance to reunite.—Kasmeer

Commander,Looks like the lighthouse you sabotaged is about to pay off! A Dead Ship has been sighted off the northern shore of orr, following the light from Beacon Torch. Orders are to rendezvous and be ready to board that ship when it runs aground. The squad's meting with Fel Vaerdenmer, one of the Pact's best captains. He'll brief you when you arrive.Agent Zrii

Commander,My congratulations and condolences: I received word on the success of your mission and the loss of our demolitions expert. We can discuss the details when you return to Fort Trinity—Zhaitan's forces are planning a major assault on our headquarters, so I need you back here as soon as possible. Until then,—Marshal Trahearne

Commander,Since you had to borrow one of my skyscales, I was thinking you might crave the opportunity to raise your own; a partner to take with you on your journeys away from the tower. We try to ethically source our whelps from eggs that have been abandoned, so your first step will be to check any abandoned skyscale nests you may find and bring me an egg. I will help guide you through the rest.You can find me at my post near the Beacon of Ages.—Dyanne

Commander,Thank you again for your help with raising the young one!Our research team has detected some anomalies related to the skyscales recently, and I could use your help looking into it. Please come see me when you can.—Dyanne

Commander,Thank you for your help during the All-Legion Rally.Blood Legion must start rebuilding the bridge to the Shiverpeaks that Bangar's troops destroyed. I'm looking for volunteers to head back up the mountain pass to see if conditions have improved enough to do so. After seeing in person what you're capable of, I figured I'd reach out to you.If you're interested, speak to my captain near Shiver Gate. Both Rytlock and I will try to stay in touch if you decide to head back up there.Best Regards,Crecia

Commander,The civil war among the charr has cost us dearly—and it's costing us more every day. But we may have an opportunity to turn the tide. Ryland's proposed a parley with the imperators and me. We can't afford to turn it down.Reach out to Rytlock; he can bring you to the front. You've got just as much at stake in this as we do.—Tribune Crecia Stoneglow

Commander,The conflict in the Crystal Desert is bleeding over into central Tyria, with refugees pouring into Lion's Arch every day. Queen Jennah has already dispatched a diplomatic team from Divinity's Reach. You and I have some common interests in play. I'd like to discuss them in private—I think we can help each other.Meet me at my office as soon as you can.—Captain Ellen Kiel

Commander,The earthquakes seem to be tapering off, but that's not much. Destroyers are still erupting all over. Every Vigil outpost has been called upon to help neighbors.Stay ready. Feels like this is only the beginning.—Jhavi
Commander,The Ebon Vanguard is stationed at the Eye of the North. We're planning our next moves and could use your help. Meet me when you can.—Jonna

Commander,The Luxons called the Jade Sea home once. Though that was a long time ago...We heard of your efforts in protecting this land. Our home. We also heard you've found yourself a siege turtle egg. We'd like to help you raise it, should you choose to keep it.Meet with my sister, Rota. I believe she's working out of Arborstone. She will guide you.—Valrun
Commander,There's something I could use your help with. Come find me at the Durmand Priory camp in Starlit Weald.

Commander,To no one's surprise, Mabon and the wizards have employed their typical cryptic form of guidance. I'm sure you're left with more questions than answers about how to proceed. To that end, I'm sending you this report on known White Mantle activity over the past several months. Hopefully this will help you track down these touchstones.You can thank me later.—Livia

Commander,Urgent Durmand Priory business.Farrah Baelseeker, our leading titan archivist, is missing. Farrah is the reliable sort, so her disappearing like this is highly out of character. I last ran into her checking out some materials here at the library. Nothing unusual about it.I'm told that you, Malice, and Caithe have been researching the titans' whereabouts. You three should have a look around Farrah's office yourselves. When you arrive, I'll have Archivist Sestina show you to Farrah's research station. Given these outstanding circumstances, you three will be granted access to the priory regardless of membership. Make haste,—Gixx

Commander,Very exciting news! A crystalline resonance cascade scenario has occurred! You can read my latest publication, "Crystalline Waveform Resonance Cascade Collapse Scenarios and You: A brief primer by award-winning Crystallographer Smoxxi" very soon, but I wanted to let you know about it right away since it centers on you!All of your red and green prophet shards and crystals are now blue! Additionally, it seems the shards localized to this area will all be blue from now on as well! Isn't that amazing? I'll still, of course, accept blue shards from you for recompense, and the chests nearby seem to function fine with this change.I assure you nothing has been lost in this scenario, and your friends may think they still have red and green shards for a time, but the moment they try to do anything with them, they will be blue. Try not to be too discombobulated—even though things have suddenly changed, they still effectively remain the same, possibly even better since you don't have to think about what color this week is now!

Commander,Very exciting news! A crystalline resonance cascade scenario has occurred! You can read my upcoming publication...wait. That book already exists; it's right here on my desk.This already happened!Okay, so, remember when all of your green prophet shards and crystals turned blue? Maybe you don't; I somehow forgot. That was the primary crystalline resonance cascade scenario—it happened a while ago! BUT! NOW! This is so amazing: it REBOUNDS! I thought the original scenario passed and was done. All my theories are wrong! This book is blissfully foul and wrong! I don't deserve this truly massive shelf full of awards and accolades! I have to go throw it all out and consult my crystals! Red prophet shards don't even exist! Why would I write about them?I'm back. This is fine. Everything is fine. I don't care what Phlunt says—crystallography has not been turned on its sixth axis at all. We're OK. This is just going to keep happening periodically and that's fine.Your green prophet shards have turned blue. Again. My associate Zazzl now collects blue shards because all the shards in Cantha are blue now. This is just the world we live in.I'm assigning another protege—very smart kid—Nikki, to go find where the locus of green shards is centering. She'll take them off your hands until the next rebound event.
Commander,We're preparing for the next battle. Return to the Eye of the North. Your army needs its commander.—Ogden Stonehealer

Commander,What a shame about demolitionist Tonn. Don't lose heart: your Wyld Hunt, and mine, still have a long way to go, and I hate the thought of going on without you.I'm about to meet up with Eir, and possibly Zojja, in yet another attempt to bridge the gap that divides us. I don't see why the others can't simply leave the past alone. Mistakes get made, people die. It's a lesson we all need to learn, and one that I hope you've already accepted.May the Pale Tree bless you and guide you,—Caithe

Commander,What happened to demolitionist Tonn was a tragedy, but you cannot let it ruin your resolve. I know something about losing those with whom you go into battle, and I won't let you make the same mistakes I made.Don't focus on what went wrong. Focus on the fact that you sank Zhaitan's flagship. That will make the Pact's ultimate victory much, much easier to achieve.I am still working to heal the rifts that keep Destiny's Edge at each other's throats, but it is slow going. Zojja is especially difficult to reach, so I'm currently focusing my efforts on her. Wish me luck.May the Spirits continue to guide you to victory,—Eir

Commander,With Zhaitan targeting both of us and Fort Trinity, it's imperative that we be prepared. I'm buttressing the fort's defenses, but I need you here to defend it against Zhaitan's next offensive. Return to Fort Trinity as soon as you can. I'll be standing by.—Marshal Trahearne

Commander,You'll find the dead drops scattered throughout Bloodtide Coast. Here are the rough locations for each drop: * Northwest of Laughing Gull Island. * Near a lookout tower on Laughing Gull Island. * Under a ship off of Jelako Cliffrise. * On a hill in the Firth of Revanion. * Near Mistarion Beach's shoreline.  Good luck.

Commander,You've earned my gratitude by helping thwart a hostile takeover. In return, I've come by some information I know will be valuable to you. I've summoned your guildmates to meet you in my casino so that all of you can hear what I've learned.—Zalambur

Commander,Your allies are struggling to reach you from Castora—the magical anomalies surrounding the island continue to scatter all communication frequencies. A nautical expedition outside of Cantha was able to parse a message for you from Tribune Rytlock, however. Your party has detained a handful of Inquest scouts, but they are holding on questioning the detainees until your arrival.Make haste.

Commander,  I'd rather not involve the wardens of the Grove in this matter, but I could use your assistance. Tensions are rising, drama is stirring... Please, with the utmost urgency, please return to our home at once.I look forward to catching up.

Commander,    Farrah Baelseeker, our leading titan archivist, is missing. Farrah is the reliable sort, so her disappearing like this is highly out of character. I last ran into her checking out some materials here at the library. Nothing unusual about it.    I'm told that you, Malice, and Caithe have been researching the titans' whereabouts. You three should have a look around Farrah's office yourselves. When you arrive, I'll have Archivist Sestina show you to Farrah's research station. Given these outstanding circumstances, you three will be granted access to the priory regardless of membership.     Make haste.  —Steward Gixx

Commander,    I have some business I'd like to discuss with you. It involves the Captain's Council, so your discretion is appreciated. Mind meeting me near the bay docks in Lion's Arch? The sooner, the better.   —Lawson

Commander. I've successfully incubated and hatched several additional fledglings. But something's not right with our original hatchling. She's lethargic, agitated, and not getting along with her siblings. This may simply be a consequence of our hand-rearing, but I'm concerned this may be something more. Please return as soon as you're able.
Commanders are disabled in quickplay squads. All players may initiate ready checks and place location and object markers.

CommanderWhat happened to your soldiers in the mortar company was a tragedy, but you cannot let it ruin your resolve. I know something about losing those with whom you go into battle, and I won't let you make the same mistakes I made.Don't focus on what went wrong. Focus on the fact that you uprooted the mesmer who caused the tragedy and in doing so, saved many more lives.I am still working to heal the rifts that keep Destiny's Edge at each other's throats, but it is slow going. Zojja is especially difficult to reach, so I'm currently focusing my efforts on her. Wish me luck.May the Spirits continue to guide you to victory,—Eir

Commander—Congratulations on handling the Fahranur incident. My intelligence suggests you did indeed slow Palawa Joko's invasion, though his incursions into Tyria continue and he's doubtless working to accelerate them. Be assured my organization stands ready to assist in stopping him.In that spirit, the Order of Shadows may also be able to shed light on some of the information Taimi has uncovered in her investigations at Fahranur, particularly her conclusion that Joko has gained control of another asura gate hub. Meet me at the Lifeblood Waterhole and I'll share some details with you.—Kito

Commission Resources
Commission[s]

Commissioned for the joint coronation of Queen Dahlah and Queen Nahlah, the last rulers of the Primeval Dynasty. The sisters adopted twin cobras as the symbol of their reign, representing the duality of venom as a deadly poison and powerful medicine.    Pairs of these large cast-metal sculptures were erected in cities and villages that hosted the queens during their royal progress and scattered during the Scarab Plague. A discarded original design depicting a single cobra with two heads was not practical for rapid assembly.
